title: Weather Update May 9 2026: Scattered Thunderstorms And Lightning To Hit Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh And Srinagar! Heatwave Horror To Trouble Central India - DNP India
full_text:
Weather Update May 9 2026: The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) has predicted that the states of Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand and Jammu & Kashmir may experience moderate to heavy snowfall accompanied by scattered thunderstorms and lightning, especially during the early morning and late evening hours. IMD also predicted that the states of Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh may experience severe heat and a possible rise of maximum temperatures by 2-5 degrees Celsius.
Delhi NCR, Uttarakhand, Jammu & Kashmir And Himachal Pradesh Weather Update
Several parts in Anantnag, Bandipora, Baramulla, Budgam, Doda, Ganderbal, Jammu, Kathua, Kishtwar, Kulgam, Kupwara, Poonch, Pulwama, Rajouri, Ramban, Reasi, Samba, Shopian, Srinagar, Udhampur, Sopore, Handwara, Bhaderwah, Tral, Awantipora, Akhnoor and Batote may experience bone-chilling weather conditions, accompanied by light to moderate rain and snowfall.
Almora, Bageshwar, Chamoli, Champawat, Dehradun, Haridwar, Nainital, Pauri Garhwal, Pithoragarh, Rudraprayag, Tehri Garhwal, Udham Singh Nagar, Uttarkashi, Rishikesh, Roorkee, Kashipur, Haldwani, Ramnagar, Kotdwar, Srinagar, Lohaghat, Tanakpur, Sitarganj, Vikasnagar, Doiwala, Herbertpur and Bhowali residents may experience challenges due to continuous rainfall and landslides.
Punjab Weather Update
Amritsar, Barnala, Bathinda, Faridkot, Fatehgarh Sahib, Fazilka, Firozpur, Gurdaspur, Hoshiarpur, Jalandhar, Kapurthala, Ludhiana, Mansa, Moga, Sri Muktsar Sahib, Pathankot, Patiala and Rupnagar are likely to experience scorching heat, with a slight possibility of light rain during evening hours.
Sahibzada Ajit Singh Nagar, Sangrur, Shahid Bhagat Singh Nagar, Tarn Taran, Abohar, Malerkotla, Khanna and Phagwara may also experience similar weather conditions.
Haryana Weather Update
Maximum temperatures may increase by 2 to 3 degrees Celsius in several parts of Ambala, Bhiwani, Charkhi Dadri, Faridabad, Fatehabad, Gurugram and Hisar.
Jhajjar, Jind, Kaithal, Karnal, Kurukshetra, Mahendragarh, Nuh, Palwal, Panchkula, Panipat, Rewari, Rohtak, Sirsa, Sonipat, Yamunanagar, Bahadurgarh, Hodal, Tohana, Gohana and Safidon could see an increase of 2 to 5 degrees Celsius in maximum temperatures.
Disclaimer: This content is based on meteorological interpretation and climatological datasets assessed by the IMD forecasting team and other sources. While we at DNP India strive to maintain scientific accuracy, weather patterns may evolve due to dynamic atmospheric conditions. This assessment is intended for informational purposes and should not be considered an absolute or guaranteed prediction.
